nodes.name: Google Drive Trigger
nodes.notes: Ingestion entry (§154). File binary flows to Init.

nodes.name: Load Config Cache

parameters.query: SELECT domain, key, value FROM configuration.configuration;

nodes.notes: R-08 runtime config. Bind CRIE Postgres/Supabase credential.

nodes.name: Load Provider Records
nodes.notes: R-08 provider records. Bind CRIE Postgres/Supabase credential.

nodes.name: Download File
nodes.notes: Downloads the file the trigger reported. fileId from Google Drive Trigger. Binary -> field 'data' (Put Output File in Field). Bind the same Google Drive OAuth credential as the trigger. Google-native docs are exported to PDF so Azure DI (SW-005) can OCR them.

nodes.name: SW-003 Duplicate Check

parameters.query: SELECT CASE WHEN COALESCE((SELECT value::text FROM configuration.configuration WHERE domain='development' AND key='development.duplicate_detection_bypass' LIMIT 1), 'false') = 'true' THEN NULL ELSE (SELECT id FROM repository.documents WHERE sha256 = '{{$json.sha256}}' LIMIT 1) END AS "dupDocumentId", '{{$json.correlationId}}' AS "correlationId", '{{$json.sha256}}'       AS "sha256", '{{$json.filename}}'     AS "filename", '{{$json.mimeType}}'     AS "mimeType", '{{$json.source}}'       AS "source";

nodes.notes: Dev-mode bypass: when configuration.configuration has domain='development', key='development.duplicate_detection_bypass', value=true (jsonb), dupDocumentId is forced NULL so the pipeline reprocesses without repo writes blocking it. Absent/false -> normal SHA-256 duplicate lookup (production behavior). Always returns one row.

nodes.name: SW-004 Register Document

parameters.query: WITH flag AS (SELECT COALESCE((SELECT value::text FROM configuration.configuration WHERE domain='development' AND key='development.duplicate_detection_bypass' LIMIT 1),'false')='true' AS bypass_enabled), existing AS (SELECT id FROM repository.documents WHERE sha256='{{$json.sha256}}' LIMIT 1), ins AS (INSERT INTO repository.documents (sha256, filename, status, authority) SELECT '{{$json.sha256}}','{{$json.filename}}','REGISTERED','Unspecified' WHERE NOT ((SELECT bypass_enabled FROM flag) AND (SELECT id IS NOT NULL FROM existing)) RETURNING id) SELECT COALESCE((SELECT id FROM ins),(SELECT id FROM existing)) AS "documentId", '{{$json.correlationId}}' AS "correlationId", '{{$json.sha256}}'       AS sha256, '{{$json.filename}}'     AS filename, '{{$json.mimeType}}'     AS "mimeType", '{{$json.source}}'       AS source;

nodes.notes: Idempotent register gated by development.duplicate_detection_bypass. flag=false (production): INSERT always runs (WHERE clause = true); unexpected duplicate still errors on uq_documents_sha256, exactly as before. flag=true + existing sha256: INSERT skipped, reuses existing document id. flag=true + new sha256: normal INSERT. Output contract unchanged.

nodes.name: SW-005 Azure OCR
nodes.notes: After import, open this node and select 'SW-005 Azure OCR' from the Workflow dropdown (canonical SW-005). The value is intentionally blank — n8n assigns the real workflow ID on your instance; it cannot be embedded in the exported JSON.

parameters.query: UPDATE repository.documents SET status='HUMAN_REVIEW' WHERE id='{{$json.documentId}}'; INSERT INTO monitoring.processing_history (document_id,stage,status,correlation_id) VALUES ('{{$json.documentId}}','OCR','FAILED','{{$json.correlationId}}'); SELECT '{{$json.correlationId}}' AS "correlationId", '{{$json.documentId}}'   AS "documentId", '{{$json.sha256}}'       AS sha256, '{{$json.filename}}'     AS filename, '{{$json.mimeType}}'     AS "mimeType", '{{$json.source}}'       AS source;

nodes.name: SW-006 OCR Validate
nodes.notes: OCR validation is PARAGRAPH-FIRST against the normalized model. A DOCX legitimately has zero pages, so validity is judged on paragraphs (the universal unit). Page confidence is checked only when the format paginates natively.

nodes.name: SW-007 LLM Metadata
nodes.notes: After import, open this node and select 'SW-007 LLM Metadata' from the Workflow dropdown (canonical SW-007). The value is intentionally blank — n8n assigns the real workflow ID on your instance; it cannot be embedded in the exported JSON.

nodes.name: SW-008 LLM Knowledge
nodes.notes: After import, open this node and select 'SW-008 LLM Knowledge' from the Workflow dropdown (canonical SW-008). The value is intentionally blank — n8n assigns the real workflow ID on your instance; it cannot be embedded in the exported JSON.

parameters.query: UPDATE repository.documents SET status='HUMAN_REVIEW' WHERE id='{{$json.documentId}}'; INSERT INTO monitoring.processing_history (document_id,stage,status,correlation_id) VALUES ('{{$json.documentId}}','KnowledgeExtraction','FAILED','{{$json.correlationId}}'); SELECT '{{$json.correlationId}}' AS "correlationId", '{{$json.documentId}}'   AS "documentId", '{{$json.sha256}}'       AS sha256, '{{$json.filename}}'     AS filename, '{{$json.mimeType}}'     AS "mimeType", '{{$json.source}}'       AS source;

nodes.name: SW-009/010/011 Evidence+Cite+Validate
nodes.notes: Pure: evidence (§32), citations R-06, validation R-05.

parameters.query: UPDATE repository.documents SET status='HUMAN_REVIEW' WHERE id='{{$json.documentId}}'; INSERT INTO monitoring.processing_history (document_id,stage,status,correlation_id) VALUES ('{{$json.documentId}}','KnowledgeExtraction','FAILED','{{$json.correlationId}}'); SELECT '{{$json.correlationId}}' AS "correlationId", '{{$json.documentId}}'   AS "documentId", '{{$json.sha256}}'       AS sha256, '{{$json.filename}}'     AS filename, '{{$json.mimeType}}'     AS "mimeType", '{{$json.source}}'       AS source;

nodes.name: SW-013 Embeddings
nodes.notes: After import, open this node and select 'SW-013 Embeddings' from the Workflow dropdown (canonical SW-013). The value is intentionally blank — n8n assigns the real workflow ID on your instance; it cannot be embedded in the exported JSON.

nodes.name: SW-014 Repository Writer (single txn)
nodes.notes: After import, open this node and select 'SW-014 Repository Writer' from the Workflow dropdown (canonical SW-014). The value is intentionally blank — n8n assigns the real workflow ID on your instance; it cannot be embedded in the exported JSON.

parameters.query: SELECT ((SELECT count(*) FROM repository.knowledge_units WHERE document_id='{{$json.documentId}}')>0) AS certified, '{{$json.correlationId}}' AS "correlationId", '{{$json.documentId}}'   AS "documentId", '{{$json.sha256}}'       AS sha256, '{{$json.filename}}'     AS filename, '{{$json.mimeType}}'     AS "mimeType", '{{$json.source}}'       AS source;

nodes.name: Re-attach File Binary
nodes.notes: Binary re-attachment. Postgres nodes drop item.binary; this restores the FULL binary object from Download File (preserving filename, mimeType, storage pointer) so SW-005 OCR receives it. json unchanged. Placed on main wire SW-004 -> SW-005; checkpoint side-branch untouched.

meta.description: Knowledge Ingestion, portable node-graph form (v1.1 §154, §210-216). Deterministic logic in Code nodes (verbatim Module 13); all external I/O (OCR/LLM/embeddings/DB/storage) in credentialed nodes and SW Execute-Workflow sub-steps. No process.env, no $env, no filesystem require, no in-Code provider/credential calls. Failure policy encoded in the graph: Duplicate->Stop, OCR->HUMAN_REVIEW, Extraction->HUMAN_REVIEW, Repository->Rollback (single txn §234). R-18 checkpoints per stage.
meta.canonicalIds: Module 13 SW-001..SW-015 (R-01) — pure steps inlined, I/O steps as SW sub-workflows
meta.portability: standard n8n 2.29.9 self-hosted; Code nodes use Web Crypto only (globalThis.crypto), NO require, NO Node builtins, NO Buffer, NO process.env/$env
changelog: v0.5.2: Google Drive Trigger emits metadata only; added 'Download File' (Google Drive download) before Init so the binary exists. Init now consumes item.binary.data from the Download node. Module 13 SW-001/002 logic unchanged.

v0.6.0: FIXED execution-stream multiplication. Config/provider Postgres lookups moved to a reference branch off the trigger (they terminate; rows never enter the main stream). Main stream = Trigger->Download->Init->... at exactly 1 item. Code nodes read config via $('...').all(). Added single-item topology guard in Init. Fan-out (if any) is internal to the item's chunks[] array; stream stays 1 item end-to-end, preserving §234 single-transaction write.

v0.6.1: Init reads binary via this.helpers.getBinaryDataBuffer(0,'data') (official n8n API), fixing InvalidCharacterError in filesystem binary-data mode. No longer assumes item.binary.data.data is inline base64; atob path kept only as legacy 'default' mode fallback. Removed raw base64 from the outgoing item (SW-005 reads binary via the same helper).

v0.6.2: Code nodes now embed module13_translated.js VERBATIM (single source of truth shared with the canonical acceptance test) + a thin per-node wrapper. Only runtime-required wrapper fixes applied: csUUID()->uuidV4(); removed dead csB64ToBytes fallback. No orchestrator/IF/Postgres/ExecuteWorkflow/SQL/business-logic changes. module.exports stripped from the embedded copy (Code nodes forbid it; the on-disk module keeps it for the test).

v0.6.3: Init binary-read fix. Root cause: the guarded if/else fell through to a misleading 'getBinaryDataBuffer is unavailable' throw when `this` wasn't bound in a test harness; the probe proved the helper IS callable in the real node (live call returned a 29MB Buffer). Replaced the guard+else-throw with a direct getBinaryDataBuffer(0,'data') call. Module 13 body byte-identical; only the binary-read lines in the Init wrapper changed.

v0.6.4: Fix `this` binding loss. Captured `const __helpers = this.helpers` as the first top-level statement; binary read uses __helpers. Module 13 byte-identical; topology untouched; only Init changed.

v0.6.5: SW-003 Duplicate Check now ALWAYS emits exactly one item. Root cause: executeQuery emits one item per returned ROW, so a no-match SELECT returned zero items and n8n killed the stream before the IF node. Fix: outer SELECT has no FROM (guaranteed one row); dup id is a scalar subquery (id or NULL); Init's fields are echoed so downstream $json.* survive. Only SW-003 changed; topology untouched.

v0.7.0: Item-propagation contract completed. All main-path Postgres nodes (SW-004 register, 4 checkpoints, SW-015 cert, 3 HUMAN_REVIEW fail nodes) now PRESERVE accumulated state by appending a trailing SELECT of the document-state fields (+ produced values documentId/certified). SQL semantics (INSERT/UPDATE/SELECT) unchanged; only a passthrough SELECT added. Init returns documentId:null placeholder. Combined with enriched SW workflows, accumulated state now survives every main-path node. No topology/orchestration/business-logic changes.

v0.7.1: Checkpoints moved OFF the main stream (side-effect logging). Each checkpoint now hangs off its predecessor as a terminating side branch; the main wire goes predecessor->next-real-node directly, so the accumulated item passes through untouched. Checkpoint INSERTs unchanged (execute once at the same stage). Reverted checkpoint passthrough-SELECT (no longer needed off-stream). No logic/stage-order/failure-policy change.

v0.7.2: Execute Workflow nodes upgraded from legacy typeVersion 1 (bare-string workflowId, no picker) to typeVersion 1.2 resource-locator format (mode:list) so each sub-workflow can be selected from the dropdown after import. value left blank by design — n8n assigns workflow IDs at import; cachedResultName shows the intended SW target. No logic/topology change.

v0.7.3: SW-003 gains a config-driven dev-mode bypass. Reads development.duplicate_detection_bypass from configuration.configuration; when true, dupDocumentId=NULL (skip lookup) for repeat integration testing; otherwise normal SHA-256 lookup. Production default (flag absent) = unchanged behavior. Still one row; passthrough columns intact. No repository changes.

v0.7.4: SW-004 Register made idempotent under dev-bypass. When development.duplicate_detection_bypass=true and the sha256 already exists, reuses the existing document id instead of INSERTing (avoids uq_documents_sha256 violation on replay). Production (flag false) behavior byte-identical: INSERT always runs and a genuine duplicate still errors. Output contract (documentId + passthrough) unchanged.
v0.7.5: SW-004 — renamed CTE boolean alias 'on' (reserved keyword) to 'bypass_enabled'. Pure syntax fix; logic identical to v0.7.4.

v0.7.6: Added 'Re-attach File Binary' Code node between SW-004 Register and SW-005 OCR. Postgres nodes (SW-003/SW-004) drop item.binary; this node restores it from Download File so SW-005 receives item.binary.data. Full binary audit confirmed only ONE re-attach needed: the sole binary consumers are node-init (SHA-256, upstream of all Postgres — safe) and SW-005 (OCR). All Postgres nodes after OCR have no downstream binary consumer. Checkpoint side-branch and all logic unchanged; one node + one edge added.

v0.7.7: Re-attach File Binary now preserves the ENTIRE binary object via $('Download File').first().binary (was reconstructing only binary.data). Keeps all binary metadata — filename, mimeType, filesystem-mode storage pointer. Mode set to runOnceForAllItems to match .first() + return-array form. No other change.

v0.8.0: SW-006 OCR validation made paragraph-first to match the normalized OCR model. The canonical page-centric check ('no pages' -> invalid) would reject every DOCX, which has no native pagination. Validity now rests on paragraphs[]; page confidence is evaluated only for paginated formats.
